mnemonics.title: Big Data 5 Vs
mnemonics.content: VIVVV: Volume (size), Velocity (speed), Variety (types), Veracity (quality), Value (usefulness)

mnemonics.title: Cloud Service Models
mnemonics.content: SI-P-F: Software (SaaS) is ready to use, Platform (PaaS) is for developers, Infrastructure (IaaS) is raw building blocks

mnemonics.title: AI Types
mnemonics.content: ANI (Narrow), AGI (General), ASI (Super) - think 'N, G, S' for Narrow to Super

acronyms.usage: Network of connected devices like smart home appliances, wearables, and industrial sensors
AI subset where systems learn from data to improve performance without explicit programming
ML technique using multi-layer neural networks for complex pattern recognition
Mobile network technology with high speed, low latency, and massive device connectivity
Enables different software applications to communicate with each other
Serverless computing model where code runs in response to events
Container orchestration platform for automating deployment and scaling of containers
Specialized processor originally for graphics, now used for parallel computing in AI/DL

visualTips.visualization: Think of a light switch (classical bit: 0 OR 1) vs a dimmer switch (qubit: 0 AND 1 simultaneously)
Edge = processing at the 'edge' (outer boundary) of the network near devices; Cloud = central processing hub far from devices
Private = your own exclusive office; Public = shared coworking space; Hybrid = combination with secure bridge between them
Input layer = receiving data; Hidden layers = processing/learning features; Output layer = giving results - like eyes (input) → brain (hidden) → action (output)
VM = complete house with its own kitchen; Container = apartment with shared kitchen facilities (more lightweight)

analogies.analogy: Like utilities (electricity, water) - you pay for what you use, don't build your own power plant
Like a security guard checking ID at the door rather than sending everyone to headquarters for verification - faster response
Two synchronized watches - knowing one tells you the time on the other instantly, regardless of distance
Like a nervous system for buildings/cities - sensors (nerves) collect info, send to brain (cloud) for decisions
Teaching a child by showing examples (data) until they recognize patterns, rather than giving rule-by-rule instructions
